name: V-Model Regulated Development Pipeline
description: Industry safety standards compliant V-model (e.g., IEC 62304, ISO 26262, DO-178C). Left side descends through requirements, design, and implementation. Right side ascends through unit testing, static analysis, integration testing, and acceptance testing -- each level validating the corresponding left-side artifact.

ASSIGN.name: Task Assignment
ASSIGN.description: Manager assigns a task with requirement IDs, acceptance criteria, and security classification.
ASSIGN.prompt:
Assign a task with regulatory traceability.

**Procedure:**
1. Use find_agents_by_role('developer') to look up the developer
2. Call send_message() with:
   - Branch name: reg/{{taskId}}
   - Acceptance criteria (testable, measurable)
   - Requirement ID(s) for traceability
   - Risk classification (per applicable industry safety standard)

Rules:
- Priority: NORMAL
- One task at a time
- Include requirement ID(s) for regulatory traceability
- Acceptance criteria must be verifiable in ACCEPTANCE_TESTING

REQUIREMENTS_ANALYSIS.name: Requirements Analysis
REQUIREMENTS_ANALYSIS.description: V-model left side (Level 1). Developer reviews requirements, decomposes them into testable specifications, and documents acceptance criteria. This is the counterpart of ACCEPTANCE_TESTING on the right side.
REQUIREMENTS_ANALYSIS.prompt:
Analyze and document the requirements for this task.

**Requirement IDs:** {{requirementIds}}
**Acceptance Criteria:** {{acceptanceCriteria}}

You are ALREADY on branch reg/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands created it from main. Do NOT create branches or run git checkout.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Create or update a requirements document at docs/requirements/{{taskId}}.md
3. For each requirement ID, document:
   - Requirement text (what is required)
   - Rationale (why)
   - Verification method (how it will be tested in ACCEPTANCE_TESTING)
   - Traceability: REQ -> acceptance test case mapping

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout main && git pull origin main
git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout reg/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Create feature branch or switch to it.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the requirements analysis just completed, were ALL of the following done: (1) docs/requirements/{{taskId}}.md created or updated with requirement text, rationale, and verification method for each requirement ID, and (2) traceability mapping documented? Answer with a single word: true or false.

DESIGN.name: System and Detailed Design
DESIGN.description: V-model left side (Level 2). Developer creates the technical design: module structure, interfaces, data flow, and error handling strategy. This is the counterpart of INTEGRATION_TESTING on the right side.
DESIGN.prompt:
Create the technical design for this task.

**Requirements Doc:** {{requirementsDoc}}
**Requirement IDs:** {{requirementIds}}

You are ALREADY on branch reg/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands checked it out. Do NOT create branches or run git checkout.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Create or update a design document at docs/design/{{taskId}}.md
3. Document:
   - Module structure (which crates, which modules)
   - Public interfaces (traits, structs, functions with signatures)
   - Data flow between modules
   - Error handling strategy (error types, Result patterns)
   - Safety considerations (unsafe usage justification if needed)
   - Integration points (how modules interact -- this drives INTEGRATION_TESTING)
   - Traceability: REQ -> design element mapping

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the design work just completed, was a design document created or updated at docs/design/{{taskId}}.md covering module structure, public interfaces, data flow, error handling, and REQ -> design traceability? Answer with a single word: true or false.

IMPLEMENTING.name: Implementation
IMPLEMENTING.description: V-model bottom. Developer writes code per the design, with unit test stubs. This is the transition point from the left side to the right side of the V.
IMPLEMENTING.prompt:
Implement the code per the design document.

**Design Doc:** {{designDoc}}
**Requirement IDs:** {{requirementIds}}

You are ALREADY on branch reg/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands checked it out. Do NOT create branches or run git checkout.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Implement the code following the design document
3. Follow validated coding practices per coding standards
4. Minimize unsafe blocks -- document any with safety comments
5. Add requirement ID comments (// REQ: {{requirementIds}}) at implementation points
6. Write unit test stubs for each public function

Quality gates (build, test, lint) run automatically as exit commands -- fix any errors the gates surface before the state can transition.

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the implementation just completed, was the code implemented per the design document with requirement ID annotations and unit test stubs for each public function? Answer with a single word: true or false.

UNIT_TESTING.name: Unit Testing
UNIT_TESTING.description: V-model right side (Level 3). QA verifies unit tests exist and pass for each public function, validating the implementation against the detailed design. Counterpart of IMPLEMENTING.
UNIT_TESTING.prompt:
Verify unit tests on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Design Doc:** {{designDoc}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Run: {{testCommand}} -- verify all tests pass
3. Run coverage measurement (cargo tarpaulin if available)
4. For each public function in the design document:
   - Verify a corresponding unit test exists
   - Verify it tests both success and error paths
5. Verify coverage >= {{coverageThreshold}}%
6. If coverage is missing for a public API or error path, write a focused test

**Pass criteria:**
- All unit tests pass
- Each public function has unit tests
- Coverage >= {{coverageThreshold}}%

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the unit testing just completed, did ALL tests pass and is coverage >= {{coverageThreshold}}% with each public function covered? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_UNIT.name: Rework (Unit Tests)
REWORK_UNIT.description: Developer adds missing unit tests or fixes failing tests.
REWORK_UNIT.prompt:
Fix unit test issues on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have checked it out.

**Test Results:** {{testResults}}
**Current Coverage:** {{coveragePercent}}%
**Required Coverage:** {{coverageThreshold}}%

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Add missing unit tests for each public function
3. Fix any failing tests
4. Ensure both success and error paths are tested
5. Run: {{testCommand}} -- verify all pass

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

STATIC_ANALYSIS.name: Static Analysis
STATIC_ANALYSIS.description: QA runs pedantic static analysis, checks unsafe usage, and audits dependencies.
STATIC_ANALYSIS.prompt:
Run static analysis on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Run: {{staticAnalysisCommand}}
3. Run: cargo audit (if available)
4. Check for unsafe blocks -- each must have a // SAFETY: comment
5. Check that no TODO or FIXME comments remain in changed files
6. Verify requirement ID comments (// REQ:) are present

**Pass criteria:**
- Zero clippy warnings (pedantic mode)
- All unsafe blocks documented with SAFETY comments
- No TODO/FIXME in changed files
- Requirement traceability comments present

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the static analysis just completed, did all checks pass with zero warnings, all unsafe blocks documented, no TODO/FIXME remaining, and requirement traceability comments present? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_STATIC.name: Rework (Static Analysis)
REWORK_STATIC.prompt:
Fix static analysis issues on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have checked it out.

**Issues:** {{staticAnalysisResults}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Address each issue from static analysis
3. Add missing SAFETY comments for unsafe blocks
4. Remove TODO/FIXME comments (implement or defer properly)
5. Run: {{staticAnalysisCommand}} -- verify clean

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

INTEGRATION_TESTING.name: Integration Testing
INTEGRATION_TESTING.description: V-model right side (Level 2). QA validates that modules integrate correctly per the design document. Counterpart of DESIGN.
INTEGRATION_TESTING.prompt:
Run integration tests on branch reg/{{taskId}} validating against the design. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Design Doc:** {{designDoc}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Review the design document for integration points
3. Run: {{testCommand}} -- include integration tests
4. Verify cross-module interactions match the design:
   - Data flows correctly between modules
   - Error propagation works across module boundaries
   - Public interfaces match design signatures
5. Verify FFI boundary if applicable

**Pass criteria:**
- All integration tests pass
- Module interactions match design document
- Error handling works across boundaries

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the integration testing just completed, did ALL integration tests pass and do module interactions match the design document? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_INTEGRATION.name: Rework (Integration)
REWORK_INTEGRATION.description: Developer fixes integration issues found when modules interact.

ACCEPTANCE_TESTING.name: Acceptance Testing
ACCEPTANCE_TESTING.description: V-model right side (Level 1). QA validates the implementation against the original requirements and acceptance criteria. Counterpart of REQUIREMENTS_ANALYSIS.
ACCEPTANCE_TESTING.prompt:
Validate against original requirements on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Requirements Doc:** {{requirementsDoc}}
**Requirement IDs:** {{requirementIds}}
**Acceptance Criteria:** {{acceptanceCriteria}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. For EACH acceptance criterion:
   - Execute the verification method documented in requirements
   - Record pass/fail with evidence
3. Build the traceability matrix:
   - Requirement -> Design element -> Source code -> Unit test -> Integration test -> Acceptance result
4. Verify each requirement ID has full traceability

**Pass criteria:**
- ALL acceptance criteria pass with evidence
- Full traceability matrix (req -> design -> code -> tests)
- No requirement left unverified

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the acceptance testing just completed, did ALL acceptance criteria pass with evidence and is the traceability matrix complete for every requirement? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_ACCEPTANCE.name: Rework (Acceptance)
REWORK_ACCEPTANCE.description: Developer fixes issues that caused acceptance test failures against requirements.

REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.name: Regulatory Sign-Off
REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.description: Manager reviews the full V-model evidence package and signs off for regulatory compliance.
REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.prompt:
Review the full V-model evidence package for regulatory sign-off.

**V-Model Evidence:**
- Requirements Analysis: {{requirementsDoc}}
- Design Document: {{designDoc}}
- Unit Testing: coverage {{coveragePercent}}%, all pass
- Static Analysis: clean (pedantic)
- Integration Testing: modules verified against design
- Acceptance Testing: all criteria verified against requirements
- Traceability: {{traceabilityMatrix}}

**Procedure:**
1. Review all QA reports for this task
2. Verify all V-model levels passed without waivers
3. Verify traceability is complete: REQ -> Design -> Code -> Unit -> Integration -> Acceptance
4. Approve merge by reporting success

If any evidence is missing or inadequate, escalate.

Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles routing.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the regulatory review just completed, is the V-model evidence package complete and adequate for sign-off (all levels passed, traceability complete, no waivers needed)? Answer with a single word: true or false.

MERGING.name: Merge to Main
MERGING.description: Fully mechanical merge. Entry commands merge the validated branch to main and capture the merge SHA. Exit commands clean up the feature branch. No LLM work required.

params.message: Branch merged to main for task {{taskId}}

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Task complete. All V-model levels validated. Code merged to main.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Task failed a V-model gate after max retries. Requires manager intervention.
ESCALATED.prompt:
This task has been escalated after failing a V-model gate.

**Options:**
1. Split into smaller tasks and re-delegate
2. Reassign to a different developer
3. Request a waiver with documented justification
4. Report blocker to user

Decide how to proceed.
